semanticCore: Boiling, bubbling heat, exaggeration, rise, or high pricing
quranicRelevance: Boiling inside bellies like oil (Yaghlee - 'yaghlee fee al-batooni', 'ka-ghalyi al-hameemi').

keyDerivations.meaning: To boil, bubble, reach boiling point, heat up, or churn
Boiling, bubbling heat, churning motion, or thermal turbulence

figurativeOrRareUsages: كَالْمُهْلِ يَغْلِي فِي الْبُطُونِ ﴿٤٥﴾ كَغَلْيِ الْحَمِيمِ (44:45-46): Like murky oil, it boils in the bellies like the boiling of scalding water (Hellfire culinary torture).
discardedNoise: Religious extremism (Ghuluw) - belonging to gh-l-w, distinct spelling (no ya).
summaryEn: The root represents boiling fluids (Ghalay), describing the painful digestion of Hell's Zaqqum tree which boils inside the bellies like scalding water.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر غليان القدر والماء شدة حرارة وتوراً (غلي)، واصفاً حركة غليان شجرة الزقوم وشراب الحميم في بطون الكفار (كغلي الحميم).
rawText: إِغْتَلَىَ 8 see 5. اغلى القِدْرَ 4 , (S, MA, K,) and 🡻غلّاهَا, (K,) He made the cooking-pot to boil. (S, * MA, K. *) IDrd mentions, among some of the sayings of the people of former ages, أُنَّ مَآءً 🡻وَغَلِّهِ [Pour thou out water, and make it to boil]. (TA.) And one says, أَغْلَيْتُ الزَّيْتَ وَنَحْوَهُ [I made the olive-oil and the like to boil], inf. n. إِغْلَآءٌ. (Msb.) تغلّى 5 (S, Msb, K) بِالغَالِيَةِ (S, Msb) He (a man, S, Msb) perfumed himself, (S, * Msb, K,) or rubbed himself over, (K,) with غَالِيَة; (S, Msb, K;) and 🡻اغتلى signifies the same; (IDrd, O in art. غلف;) as also تَغَلَّلَ, (Msb, and O in art. غلف,) and اِغْتَلَّ. (O in that art.) تَغَاْلَىَ 6 in all its sense, belongs to art. غلو, q. v. غَالِيَةٌ [Galia moschata,] a sort of perfume, (S, K,) well known; (K;) a certain compound of perfumes; (Msb;) musk mixed or boiled [with other perfumes]; (MA;) or a perfume composed of musk and ambergris and camphor and oil of ben: (KL:) it is said that the first who called it thus was Suleymán Ibn-'Abd-El-Melik; (S, TA;) and he did so because it is a compound boiled together upon the fire: or it was thus named by Mo'áwiyeh; the case being, that 'Abd-Allah Ibn-Jaafar went in to him, and the odour of perfume was diffusing itself from him; so he said, “ What is thy perfume, O 'Abd-Allah? ” and he answered, “ musk and ambergris combined with oil of ben; ” whereupon Mo'áwiyeh said, غَالِيَةٌ, meaning highpriced: (TA:) [hence some hold the word to belong to art. غلو; and their opinion is strengthened by the fact that] غَلْوَى signifies the same. (K in art. غلو.) غَلَانِيَةٌ , mentioned in the K in this art., belongs to art. غلو, being an inf. n. of the verb in the phrase غَلَا فِى الأَمْرِ. (TA.) غَلَتِ القِدْرُ 1 , aor. تَغْلِى, inf. n. غَلْىٌ and غَلَيَانٌ, (S, MA, Msb, K,) The cooking-pot boiled; (MA, &c.;) and غَلِيَت, aor. تَغْلَى, is an unusual dial. var. thereof, the former being the more chaste; (Msb;) or غَلِيَت is not allowable. (S.) === [Hence غَلَى said of a liquor, It estuated: it effervesced: it fermented: see نَبِيذٌ === And hence] يَغْلِى دَمُهُ [as though meaning † His blood is fit to be shed] is a phrase like رَابَ دَمُهُ, [q. v.], said of one who has exposed himself to slaughter: his blood being likened to milk that has become thick, and fit to be churned. (A in art. روب.) === And غَلِىَ الرَّجُلُ, like رَضِىَ in measure [but see what has been said of this form of the verb above], ‡ The man became vehemently angry. (IKtt, TA.) غَلَّايَةٌ A vessel of copper [or brass], in which water is heated; thus called by the people of Syria; the same that is called مِحَمٌّ [q. v.] and قُمْقُمٌ and قُمْقُمَةٌ. (Msb voce قُمْقُمٌ.) غَلَّىَ 2 see 4, in two places. ___ غلّى الرَّجُلَ, inf. n. تَغْلِيَةٌ, He rubbed the man over, or perfumed him, with غَالِيَة. (TA.) And غلّى لِحْيَتَهُ (Mgh, O,) Msb, all in art. غلف) بِالغَالِيَةِ (O, ibid.) He daubed, or smeared, his beard with غَالِيَة; as also غَلَّلَهَا. (Mgh, O, Msb, ibid. [See 1 in art. غلف.]) ___ And تَغْلِيَةٌ signifies also The saluting from afar, and making a sign. (K.)
